# Code Mentor — Frontend Design Preview Workspace

**What this is:** an isolated workspace where we collect UI redesigns generated by [`claude.ai/design`](https://claude.ai/design), review them, and decide page-by-page whether to apply them to the real `frontend/`.

**Hard rule:** the real `frontend/` directory **is not touched** until a Pillar is approved after a live walkthrough. This workspace exists precisely so we never repeat the 2026-04-27 rollback (ADR-030).

---

## Why a separate workspace?

| Past attempt | What happened | The fix |
|---|---|---|
| **2026-04-27** `/ui-ux-refiner` pass | Brief approved → code landed in `frontend/` → live walkthrough → owner rejected → 4-5 hour rollback (ADR-030 superseded itself the same day) | Generate the design in **isolation** first. Real `frontend/` only changes after live walkthrough sign-off. |

The lesson: **brief approval ≠ visual approval.** A design that reads great in a description can feel wrong in motion. So this workspace acts as a quarantine — anything in here is "draft" until we walk through it together in a browser.

---

## Pillar-by-pillar workflow (the one we agreed on)

We work through 8 pillars in order. Each pillar:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. I write a detailed prompt → prompts/pillar-N-name.md           │
│  2. You paste the prompt into claude.ai/design                     │
│  3. claude.ai/design outputs React + Tailwind .tsx files           │
│  4. You save the output → pillar-N-name/                           │
│  5. We run the preview in isolation → live walkthrough together    │
│  6. You log feedback → walkthrough-notes.md                        │
│  7. Decision: approve, iterate, or reject                          │
│  8. ONLY if approved → we move to the next pillar                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Approval of one pillar does NOT auto-apply to `frontend/`. We apply **all approved pillars together** as one tracked sprint after the full preview is done (or after enough pillars are approved that integration makes sense).

---

## Quick start

### Step 1 — read the canonical brief

Open [`DESIGN-BRIEF.md`](DESIGN-BRIEF.md). This is the master reference for every prompt. **The brand identity in this brief is non-negotiable** (per memory + ADR-030).

### Step 2 — start with Pillar 1

Open [`prompts/pillar-1-foundation.md`](prompts/pillar-1-foundation.md). The prompt is **already written and ready to copy-paste**.

Then:

1. Go to https://claude.ai/design
2. Start a new session
3. Paste the entire prompt
4. Wait for the output (a single design-system showcase page)
5. Save the generated `.tsx` file(s) into [`pillar-1-foundation/`](pillar-1-foundation/) — name them e.g. `DesignSystemShowcase.tsx`
6. (Optional) save a screenshot too if claude.ai/design provides one — `pillar-1-foundation/screenshot.png`

### Step 3 — preview the output locally

We'll set up a side preview server on a separate port (5174) so it never collides with the running `frontend/` (5173). Process:

```powershell
# from the project root
cd "frontend-design-preview\pillar-1-foundation"
# (a per-pillar preview folder will get a minimal Vite + Tailwind scaffold
#  on first walkthrough — I'll set that up when we get there.)
npm install
npm run dev -- --port 5174
```

(I'll generate the scaffold for whichever pillar we walk through first — no need to do this manually now.)

### Step 4 — live walkthrough (together)

Open `localhost:5174`. Click through:

- Light mode AND dark mode toggle
- Every interactive element (hover, click, focus)
- Mobile viewport (DevTools, 375px)
- Tablet (768px)
- Desktop (1280px)
- `prefers-reduced-motion` simulation in DevTools

Then I'll capture your reactions in [`walkthrough-notes.md`](walkthrough-notes.md).

### Step 5 — decide

Three outcomes:

| Outcome | What it means | What happens next |
|---|---|---|
| ✅ **Approve** | The pillar matches the identity and feels right | Move to next pillar's prompt |
| 🔁 **Iterate** | Mostly right, specific tweaks needed | I refine the prompt → you re-run claude.ai/design → re-walkthrough |
| ❌ **Reject** | Direction is off | We discuss why → I rewrite the prompt from scratch with the new constraint |

**Total surfaces:** 34 (29 pages + 4 layouts + Notifications dropdown).

---

## How to talk to `claude.ai/design`

Every prompt in `prompts/` is structured as:

```
1. Brand identity recap (condensed from DESIGN-BRIEF.md)
2. The pages to design in this pillar
3. Per-page: layout, content, states, edge cases
4. Output format requirements
5. Anti-patterns to avoid
6. Reference inspiration (where applicable)
```

You should be able to paste the whole prompt as-is. If `claude.ai/design` asks clarifying questions, **answer with the relevant section from `DESIGN-BRIEF.md`** rather than making up new constraints.

**Don't argue with the tool.** If it offers a "modernized" alternative palette or font, decline and re-anchor it to the brief.

---

## Live walkthrough checklist (run for every pillar)

Save your observations in [`walkthrough-notes.md`](walkthrough-notes.md) under the relevant pillar section. The checklist:

### Identity match
- [ ] Uses the brand trio (violet primary, cyan secondary, fuchsia accent) — NO emerald-only, no slate-only
- [ ] Uses Inter (sans) + JetBrains Mono (code) — NO Geist, NO Roboto
- [ ] Uses glass surfaces where appropriate (header, cards, modals)
- [ ] Uses neon accents *sparingly* (CTA hover, focus, status — NOT body content)
- [ ] Has the `AnimatedBackground` on public/auth pages
- [ ] Uses lucide-react icons throughout — no other icon libraries

### Accessibility
- [ ] Text contrast meets AA in both modes
- [ ] Focus-visible ring on every interactive element
- [ ] Semantic HTML (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<header>`, etc.)
- [ ] Reduced motion respected (test in DevTools)
- [ ] Color is not the only signal for error / success

### Responsive
- [ ] 320px (mobile) — content readable, no horizontal scroll
- [ ] 768px (tablet) — sensible grid breakpoints
- [ ] 1280px (desktop) — uses available space without looking stretched

### Both modes
- [ ] Light mode looks polished (not just "dark mode flipped")
- [ ] Dark mode shows the radial gradient bg
- [ ] Toggle works cleanly (no flash, no broken colors)

### Interactions
- [ ] Hover states on every interactive surface
- [ ] Loading states (skeletons, spinners) shown for async content
- [ ] Empty states have lucide icon + heading + body + action
- [ ] Form fields show validation errors inline

### Content quality
- [ ] Mock data feels realistic (real-looking names, code, scores) — not "Lorem ipsum"
- [ ] Microcopy is technical but human (no marketing fluff, no "Welcome to your new dashboard!")
- [ ] Errors are honest and actionable

---

## After full approval — applying to `frontend/`

Once enough pillars are approved (could be all 8, could be just the defense-critical 5), we'll:

1. **Append a new ADR** to `docs/decisions.md` superseding ADR-030 with the approved direction.
2. **Add a new Sprint** to `docs/implementation-plan.md` — "Sprint 13: UI Redesign Application" — with one task per pillar's integration.
3. **Run `/project-executor start sprint 13`** — that follows the normal sprint discipline (test bar, code-quality gates, progress tracking).

This ensures the integration itself is a tracked, reviewable change — not a 4000-line drop.

**Timing note:** the integration sprint runs **after** the supervisor rehearsals (S11-T12, S11-T13) so the rehearsals demo the stable current `frontend/`. The redesign goes live for post-defense polish, OR if time permits before defense, as a separate hardening pass.

---

## Files in this workspace

```
frontend-design-preview/
├── README.md                   ← you are here
├── DESIGN-BRIEF.md             ← brand identity master reference (canonical)
├── walkthrough-notes.md        ← rolling log of feedback per pillar
├── prompts/
│   ├── pillar-1-foundation.md  ← ready to use
│   └── (pillars 2-8 written incrementally after each is approved)
├── pillar-1-foundation/        ← put claude.ai/design output here
├── pillar-2-public-auth/
├── pillar-3-onboarding/
├── pillar-4-core-learning/
├── pillar-5-feedback-ai/
├── pillar-6-profile-cv/
├── pillar-7-secondary/
└── pillar-8-admin/
```

---

## What this workspace will NOT do

- **It will not modify `frontend/`.** Even if you accidentally approve something we later regret, nothing in here touches the running app.
- **It will not change `tailwind.config.js`.** Token changes happen during the integration sprint, after walkthrough.
- **It will not run in production.** This is a quarantine area for design previews only.
- **It will not be committed to the public GitHub repo.** Add `frontend-design-preview/` to the public mirror's `.gitignore` if you want — it's draft artifact territory.

---

## When you're ready

Open [`prompts/pillar-1-foundation.md`](prompts/pillar-1-foundation.md), copy the whole prompt, and paste it into a new session on https://claude.ai/design. Drop the output into `pillar-1-foundation/`. Then tell me "Pillar 1 output is ready" and we'll set up the preview server for the live walkthrough.
